title: HS - Access Denied
description: Fast-paced cybersecurity game where players identify and improve weak passwords under time pressure, reinforcing secure decision-making and MFA concepts.
fullDescription: Access Denied is an interactive cybersecurity game designed to teach players how to create secure passwords and understand multi-factor authentication (MFA) through gameplay. The experience is built around fast-paced decision-making, where weak passwords fall dynamically on screen and players must quickly select the most secure alternative from multiple options. The game also includes an MFA module based on rapid matching mechanics, reinforcing security concepts through interaction and feedback.
learnings: This project established the foundation for future developments, both technically and conceptually. It provided hands-on experience in setting up the development environment, structuring UI systems, and defining reusable components. It also introduced iterative workflows through user testing and demos, shaping an early understanding of how feedback drives continuous improvement.

title: HS - Clean Up
description: Strategy-based game focused on identifying and eliminating sensitive data using the correct methods, emphasizing prioritization and decision-making.
fullDescription: Clean Up is a strategy-based cybersecurity game inspired by tower defense mechanics, where players must identify and eliminate different types of sensitive data using the correct methods. The gameplay emphasizes prioritization and decision-making, offering a slower-paced and more thoughtful experience. The design focuses on clarity and core interactions, guiding players to understand how different types of information should be handled.
learnings: This project marked a shift towards more structured and scalable development practices. It involved simplifying complex ideas into focused experiences while improving clarity and pacing. Additionally, it led to the creation of reusable templates and standardized workflows, significantly improving development efficiency across subsequent projects.

title: HS - The Rescue
description: Arcade-style game where players classify incoming files as malicious or safe in real time, combining reflex-based gameplay with cybersecurity learning.
fullDescription: The Rescue is an arcade-style cybersecurity game inspired by classic shooters like 1942, where players must identify and classify incoming files as malicious or safe in real time. The gameplay combines reflex-based interaction with decision-making under pressure, reinforced through contextual descriptions and scoring systems. The experience also includes support mechanics such as an antivirus power-up and a lightweight in-game assistant that provides guidance and context during gameplay.
learnings: As the most mature project, it consolidated previous knowledge into a more efficient and refined development process. It emphasized cleaner execution, faster iteration, and better use of tools, including mobile-oriented add-ons. It also introduced the integration of narrative elements into gameplay, expanding the scope of interactive design.
